# services.openssh

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.openssh`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.openssh.enable` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable Apple's built-in OpenSSH server. The default is null which means let macOS manage the OpenSSH server. |
| `services.openssh.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration text loaded in {file}`sshd_config`. See {manpage}`sshd_config(5)` for help. |
| `services.openssh.hostKeys` | `list of (submodule)` | SSH host key declarations. Each entry specifies a key type and path. `HostKey` directives are written to the sshd configuration for each entry. The default matches the keys that macOS automatically generates. |
| `services.openssh.hostKeys.*.bits` | `null or signed integer` | Key size in bits. If `null`, `ssh-keygen` uses the default for the given key type (RSA=3072, ECDSA=256, ED25519=fixed). |
| `services.openssh.hostKeys.*.comment` | `string` | Comment for the key, passed to `ssh-keygen -C`. Defaults to an empty string to match Apple's built-in host key generation and avoid leaking the hostname. |
| `services.openssh.hostKeys.*.path` | `string` | Path to the private key file. |
| `services.openssh.hostKeys.*.type` | `one of "dsa", "ecdsa", "ed25519", "rsa"` | Key type passed to `ssh-keygen -t`. |
